dashboard.updateHint: This checks the official XTLS/Xray-core release feed only. It does not download or install updates automatically.

readiness.title: Readiness Center
readiness.subtitle: Aggregate config, subscriptions, probing, node pool, TUN, and update state in one place so the blocking issues are obvious first.

overview.healthyBody: The critical paths are available. Minor reminders may still exist, but they are not blocking the main workflow.
overview.warningBody: {count} warning checks need attention before they grow into failures.
overview.blockingBody: {count} blocking checks mean at least one critical path is unavailable right now.
overview.unavailableBody: The aggregate status could not be fetched yet. Refresh the page or verify the backend endpoint.

unknown.summary: An unknown readiness check was returned.

configPath.title: Config File Path
configPath.missing: webpanel.configPath is not configured, so subscriptions, node-pool management, and transparent-mode diagnostics cannot initialize fully.
configPath.notFound: The config file could not be found: {path}
configPath.invalid: The config file cannot be read or is not valid JSON: {path}

configSections.title: Required Panel Sections
configSections.ok: The api, stats, and policy sections required by the panel are present.
configSections.unavailable: The config file itself is unavailable, so the required sections cannot be checked further.
configSections.missingSectionsSummary: The config is missing core sections required by the panel.
configSections.partialSummary: The config exists, but some statistics or API service settings are still incomplete.

subscriptions.title: Subscriptions and Imports
subscriptions.unavailable: The subscription manager is not initialized, so node imports and pool maintenance are unavailable.
subscriptions.empty: There are no subscription sources yet, so the pool cannot replenish nodes automatically.
subscriptions.noNodes: {subscriptions} subscription source(s) are configured, but no nodes have been imported yet.
subscriptions.ok: {subscriptions} subscription source(s) and {nodes} node(s) are loaded.
subscriptions.poolDetail: Pool distribution: active {active}, staging {staging}, candidate {candidate}, quarantine {quarantine}, removed {removed}.

probing.title: Node Prober
probing.unavailable: The prober dependencies are unavailable, so real fail-rate and latency signals cannot be collected.
probing.notStarted: The subscription manager has not entered its probing loop yet.
probing.dispatcherUnavailable: The Xray routing dispatcher is unavailable, so node probing is disabled.
probing.idle: The prober is configured, but there are no probeable node tags right now.
probing.stopped: The prober has configuration, but it is not currently running.
probing.running: The prober is running and checks {tagCount} node tag(s) every {intervalSec} seconds.

nodePool.title: Node Pool Health
nodePool.unavailable: The node pool is currently unavailable.
nodePool.empty: The node pool does not contain any nodes yet.
nodePool.belowMinimum: The active pool has only {active} node(s), below the minimum of {minimum}.
nodePool.ok: The active pool has {active} node(s), meeting the minimum of {minimum}.
nodePool.distributionDetail: Pool distribution: active {active}, candidate {candidate}, staging {staging}, quarantine {quarantine}, removed {removed}.

tun.title: Transparent Mode / TUN
tun.unavailable: The transparent-mode manager is not initialized.
tun.degraded: Transparent mode is degraded or in an error state. Fix it before enabling it again.
tun.warning: Transparent mode has warnings. Repair privilege or helper issues before enabling it.
tun.helperMissingDetail: The helper script is missing, so transparent-mode switching cannot run.
tun.privilegeRecommendedDetail: Reinstalling or repairing the privilege helper is recommended.

updates.title: Release Check
updates.unavailable: The release checker is currently unavailable.
updates.stale: The page is showing cached release data because the latest refresh did not succeed.
updates.updateAvailable: Current version {currentVersion}; newer version {latestVersion} is available.
updates.ok: Current version {currentVersion} is up to date based on the latest known release state.

inbounds.title: Inbound Management
inbounds.deleteConfirm: Are you sure to delete this inbound?

outbounds.title: Outbound Management
outbounds.deleteConfirm: Are you sure to delete this outbound?

users.title: User Management
users.deleteConfirm: Are you sure to delete this user?

dns.title: DNS Configuration
dns.runtimeSplitBody: When transparent mode is enabled, DNS is handled separately: China-matched domains use China DNS and stay direct, while other domains use remote DNS and go through the proxy pool.
dns.remoteDnsControlHint: The remote DNS list now lives in the Node Pool page under transparent-mode settings. This page remains the explanation view for current DNS behavior.
dns.baseConfigHint: This section shows the dns block from the main config file. The actual split-DNS rules used by transparent mode are generated automatically at runtime. Edit the base dns block on the Config page.
dns.flowCnBody: Domains that match China rules are resolved through China DNS first, and both the resolution result and the follow-up traffic stay direct.
dns.flowRemoteBody: Domains that do not match China rules are resolved through remote DNS and then forwarded through the active node pool.
dns.flowBaseConfigBody: This page explains and displays DNS behavior. It is not a standalone DNS editor. The base config is still maintained on the Config page.
dns.runtimeStatusRunning: Transparent mode is running now, so the split-DNS behavior above is live.
dns.runtimeStatusStopped: Transparent mode is not running now. The split-DNS behavior above will be generated the next time transparent mode is enabled.
dns.notLoaded: DNS config is not loaded. Check the Config page.

settings.title: Settings
settings.tunSafetyDesc: This mode starts a separate TUN runtime and keeps the current WebPanel alive so the control plane does not cut itself off during switching.
settings.tunPrivilegeDesc: The panel uses sudo -n to call the root helper. Use the install action below to open a native system auth dialog and finish the helper plus sudo setup automatically.
settings.tunInstallDesc: This opens a native system authentication dialog and installs or upgrades the helper, system binary, and exact sudo rules for this machine.
settings.tunHelperMissing: The TUN helper script is missing, so transparent mode cannot be switched.
settings.tunRepairDesc: The installed helper or TUN xray does not match the repo version. Repair it before enabling transparent mode so clean restore can recover DNS safely.
settings.primaryControlsTitle: Primary controls live in Node Pool
settings.primaryControlsDesc: Transparent mode enablement, restore-clean, and pool safety decisions now live on the Node Pool page. This tab stays diagnostic-only.

subscriptions.title: Subscription Management
subscriptions.fileImportHint: The panel imports the uploaded file content once and does not keep watching the local file.
subscriptions.manualPlaceholder: Paste one share link per line, or paste the full Base64 subscription content.
subscriptions.contentRequired: Please paste subscription content or choose a local file
subscriptions.deleteConfirm: Are you sure to delete this subscription and all its nodes?
subscriptions.nonUrlEditHint: For manual and file imports, this edit dialog only changes the remark. Recreate the source if you need to replace the imported content.

nodePool.title: Node Pool
nodePool.subtitle: Validate first, enter the pool later; unstable nodes leave automatically or manually.
nodePool.bulkRemoveSelectedCandidatesConfirm: Move the selected {count} candidate nodes into removed state?
nodePool.quarantineConfirm: Move this node out of the active pool?
nodePool.removeConfirm: Move this node into removed state?
nodePool.restoreConfirm: Move this removed node back into the candidate pool?
nodePool.purgeRemovedConfirm: Permanently delete this removed node? This cannot be undone.
nodePool.deleteConfirm: Are you sure to delete this node?
nodePool.lifecycleGuideHint: Check which pool a node is in first, then decide whether to wait for probing, move it back to candidate, or leave it removed.
nodePool.fullFailureCleanupHint: Remove every non-removed node whose fail rate is 100% and whose probe count has reached the threshold. A conservative threshold like 20 or 50 is recommended.
nodePool.fullFailureCleanupConfirm: Remove the current {count} nodes with a 100% failure rate?
nodePool.bulkPurgeSelectedRemovedConfirm: Permanently delete the selected {count} removed nodes? This cannot be undone.
nodePool.bulkPurgeRemovedFullFailuresConfirm: Permanently delete these {count} removed nodes with a 100% failure rate? This cannot be undone.
nodePool.emptyEvents: No trust events yet. Import nodes or wait for validation to begin.
nodePool.emptyActive: No active nodes are ready yet.
nodePool.emptyStaging: No nodes are currently under validation.
nodePool.emptyCandidate: No candidate nodes are waiting for registration.
nodePool.candidateSectionHint: Candidate nodes are not probed automatically. Click "Send To Validation" to start probing them.
nodePool.validationPoolZeroHintFast: Min samples is set to {minSamples}, so successful nodes usually leave validation almost immediately.
nodePool.validationPoolZeroHintCandidate: There are {count} candidate nodes waiting here. They will only appear in validation after you send them there.
nodePool.validationGuideSummary: Recommended starting point: minimum active nodes 3, min samples 5, max fail rate 0.30, max average delay 1000ms, demote after 5 consecutive failures, probe every 60 seconds, keep the probe URL at https://www.gstatic.com/generate_204, and leave auto-remove demoted off at first.
nodePool.validationGuideRuleOfThumb: For a stricter pool, lower max fail rate and max average delay, and raise min samples. For faster decisions, lower min samples or shorten the probe interval.
nodePool.validationGuideMinActiveNodes: How many active nodes transparent mode should keep at minimum. For a single machine, 2 to 3 is typical; use 3 if you want more headroom.
nodePool.validationGuideMinSamples: How many probe results a node needs before it is judged. Lower values converge faster; higher values are steadier. Usually 5 to 10.
nodePool.validationGuideMaxFailRate: The highest allowed failure ratio. 0.30 means 30%. For a stable pool, 0.20 to 0.30 is a good range.
nodePool.validationGuideMaxAvgDelay: Average latency ceiling. 800 to 1500ms is common. Lower it if you want faster nodes; raise it if you want to keep more nodes.
nodePool.validationGuideDemoteAfterFails: How many consecutive failures an active node can have before it is moved out. Usually 3 to 5.
nodePool.validationGuideProbeInterval: How often probing runs. 30 to 60 seconds is usually enough. Going too short adds extra probe traffic and noise.
nodePool.validationGuideProbeUrl: This must be a full URL, not just a domain. Keeping https://www.gstatic.com/generate_204 is the safest default.
nodePool.validationGuideMinBandwidthKbps: This bandwidth threshold is mainly reserved for future use. Leave it at 0 unless you have a specific reason.
nodePool.validationGuideAutoRemoveDemoted: When enabled, demoted nodes go straight into the removed pool. Leave it off if you want to review and restore nodes manually.
nodePool.transparentRoutingConfig: Transparent Routing Policy and Direct Whitelist
nodePool.transparentRoutingDesc: Configure how transparent mode chooses nodes for external traffic, and which domains/IPs must stay direct.
nodePool.recommendedConfigBody: The default recommendation is "Auto-test Direct vs Proxy" with "Fastest First". This keeps stable base direct rules on direct paths, and automatically falls back unstable or unreachable targets to proxy. Switch back to "Strict Proxy Priority" only when you need the most conservative behavior.
nodePool.transparentRoutingRunningHint: Transparent mode is currently running. Saved policy and whitelist changes take full effect the next time transparent mode is enabled.
nodePool.transparentRoutingWhitelistHint: Domains are written to webpanel.tun.protectDomains, and IP/CIDR entries are written to webpanel.tun.protectCidrs. Built-in localhost, private CIDRs, and common local suffixes still stay direct. You can also enter *.example.com and it will be saved as domain:example.com.
nodePool.remoteDnsGuideBody: This list is used for foreign or other domains in transparent mode. You can add multiple remote DNS entries, and every entry is written into runtime in the exact order you configure. The panel no longer trims the list based on local-network probing, so the behavior stays closer to the real proxy-exit path and lets you control priority manually.
nodePool.routeModeAutoHint: Before enabling transparent mode, the panel probes base-config direct domains/IPs with a cached direct-connect check. Successful candidates stay direct; failed ones fall back to proxy. The first enable or a stale-cache refresh can take a little longer.
nodePool.remoteDnsPlaceholder:
One remote DNS per line, for example:
1.1.1.1
8.8.8.8
https://dns.google/dns-query
nodePool.protectDomainsPlaceholder:
One rule per line, for example:
full:example.com
domain:example.cn
*.example.org
nodePool.protectCidrsPlaceholder:
One IP or CIDR per line, for example:
203.0.113.10
203.0.113.0/24
nodePool.tunSettingsSavedRunning: Transparent settings saved. Restore clean mode and enable transparent mode again for full effect on the running TUN runtime
nodePool.minActiveNodes: Minimum active nodes required for transparent mode
nodePool.removedSectionHint: Removed nodes stay out of validation and transparent mode until you move them back into candidate or permanently delete their records.

lifecycleGuide.candidate: Waiting to be re-registered or moved back manually from removed; it only starts probing after you send it to validation.
lifecycleGuide.staging: Already in the validation flow. The panel tests fail rate and average latency against the probe URL.
lifecycleGuide.active: Passed validation and entered the active pool, so it can be used for transparent mode and node selection.
lifecycleGuide.quarantine: Failed repeated probes or was moved out manually; it is kept out of the active pool for now.
lifecycleGuide.removed: Removed manually or because the whole subscription was deleted. It is not probed until you move it back to candidate.

intelligenceReason.exit_ip_unavailable: No usable exit IP is available
intelligenceReason.hosting_keyword_match: Matched a hosting or cloud-provider signal
intelligenceReason.residential_keyword_match: Matched an access-ISP or residential signal
intelligenceReason.probe_failure_rate_high: Probe failure rate is too high
intelligenceReason.residential_stable_exit: Residential-like exit with stable probe history
intelligenceReason.datacenter_exit_network: Exit looks more like a datacenter network
intelligenceReason.insufficient_signal: Signal is still too weak for a verdict

reason.probe_qualified: Validation passed and entered the active pool
reason.manual_quarantine: Moved out of the active pool manually
reason.startup_default_clean: Startup defaults to a clean system
reason.startup_status_unavailable: Startup could not verify clean system status
reason.enable_blocked_min_active_not_met: Enablement blocked because the active pool is too small
reason.pool_below_min_active_nodes: Active nodes dropped below the minimum
reason.automatic_fallback_min_active_not_met: Automatically returned to clean because the active pool became unsafe
reason.state_load_defaulted: Machine state file was reset to defaults

banner.cleanTitle: The machine is clean right now
banner.proxiedTitle: Transparent mode is currently using the node pool
banner.poolWarningTitle: The active pool is not stable enough yet
banner.degradedTitle: The machine is degraded and needs attention
